description: Synthetic Python eval corpus. Primary purpose: lock in the test-path penalty for Python's filename convention (test_*.py discovered alongside source, NOT under a tests/ dir). Before the 2026-05-25 fix, co-located test_*.py files got ZERO test penalty and outranked the canonical module whenever no strong def-site rescued it (the dogfooding miss). py-penalty-001 is the hard regression guard (proven pre-fix FAIL / post-fix PASS on the hook path); the remaining cases cover ordinary Python symbol/multiword/fuzzy/negative retrieval. The eval corpus was JS + Swift only before this fixture, so Python retrieval regressions passed npm run test:eval and the swift external gate silently. Run with: node bin/intel.js scan --root fixtures/python-eval --force; node scripts/eval-retrieve.js --root fixtures/python-eval --dataset fixtures/python-eval/eval-dataset.json; node scripts/eval-hook.js --root fixtures/python-eval --dataset fixtures/python-eval/eval-dataset.json

cases.notes: B3 CONSTANT-EDGE GUARD (re-purposed 2026-05-29). app/__init__.py re-exports FLAG_REGISTRY through its __all__ barrel; the query must surface the DEFINING module (feature_gate.py) at rank 1, NOT the barrel. Fail-pre/pass-post across the re-export-kind fix stack: revert graph-retrieve's explicit->reexport_chain routing OR the merge fusion-bonus exclusion OR the python_ast.py AnnAssign capture, and the barrel (or its zoekt re-export line) outranks the def -> feature_gate.py drops below rank 1 -> FAIL. HISTORY: this case originally guarded the test-path penalty while FLAG_REGISTRY was signal-less. The AnnAssign export fix (python_ast.py now captures `NAME: T = {...}`) makes FLAG_REGISTRY a `const` export that is floored, so the def now wins via the floor rather than the penalty — this case no longer isolates the test-path penalty. The test-path penalty is now hard-locked solely by test/merge-results.test.js (penalty-sensitive: reverting C.TEST_PENALTY fails 2 of its assertions). See [[project_test_path_penalty_per_language_gap]] and [[project_real_repo_retrieval_failures]].

Fuzzy single-word query — the closest analog to the dogfooding 'flags flipped' miss. feature_gate.py is the canonical flag module but its filename does NOT contain 'flag', while two co-located test_*.py files do. The penalty effect is visible in the CLI graph-OFF arm: revert the regex and the graph-OFF ranking flips to test_flag_rollout.py #1 (graph-ON keeps feature_gate.py #1 via its fan-in=3 boost, so this is a softer signal than py-penalty-001). Kept as a realistic fuzzy-query case, NOT relied on as the hard guard.

Control case. A class def gets the +65% def-site/exact-symbol stack, so broker.py ranks #1 even though test_broker.py is co-located. Confirms the test penalty does NOT over-fire and bury a symbol that has a genuine strong definition signal.
Control case. router.py defines both add_route and route; the literal 'def route' must reach router.py's method def at rank #1.

HARD REGRESSION GUARD for A4 (natural-language recall collapse). The four query tokens appear SCATTERED in app/notifications.py and the filename contains none of them, so the multi-token query becomes a zoekt PHRASE query (quoteIfPhrase) that matches nothing, and the graph filename layer cannot reach the file. Pre-fix the canonical file is ABSENT from the candidate set (recall 0 -> FAIL via minRecall). The zero-hit AND-fallback in lib/zoekt.js:searchFast re-issues the query unquoted (whitespace-AND), which content-matches notifications.py -> rank 1 (recall 1.0, MRR 1.0 -> PASS). minRecall/maxPrimaryRank are the gate fields added to eval-hook.js for exactly this class (usefulness alone never inspects recall, which is why A4 shipped silently). REQUIRES a warm zoekt index — graph-only mode cannot recover this. See [[project_real_repo_retrieval_failures]].

B3 structural guard (def-over-barrel). RequestRouter is defined in app/router.py and re-exported through the app/__init__.py barrel. The query must surface the DEFINING module at rank 1, not the re-export shim. On a large real repo the merge layer evicted the def in favor of the barrel because graph base scores (~100) lose to a zoekt-corroborated barrel line (~500); lib/merge-results.js:DEF_SCORE_FLOOR floors canonical-def graph signals so the def competes. NOTE: at this fixture's tiny scale the def line is inside zoekt's hit cap, so this case does NOT reliably fail pre-fix (the def is not evicted when the corpus is small). It is a soft structural guard; the HARD, scale-independent B3 lock is the unit test in test/merge-results.test.js.
Garbage query across the Python corpus. Must return zero results.
